channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump sets records with pace of appointments, but that doesn't mean the transition is going smoothly
item.description: In the two weeks since Election Day, President-elect Donald Trump has been setting records with the pace of appointments for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance will introduce Gaetz, Hegseth to senators this week
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance will make the rounds with GOP senators later this week to introduce them to former-Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) and Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s picks to head the Justice and Defense departments.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Matt Gaetz 'working the phones,' speaking to GOP senators despite difficult confirmation odds
item.description: Matt Gaetz has been making calls to Republican senators ahead of a likely difficult confirmation process in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from the AP's investigation into Osprey safety issues
item.description: After being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight service members in Japan, the V-22 Osprey — a complicated aircraft that flies fast like a plane but converts to land like a helicopter — is back in the air. But there are still questions as to whether it should be.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, KEVIN VINEYS and AARON KESSLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Democrat on House Ethics panel says Gaetz report should be released
item.description: Rep. Susan Wild (D-Pa.), the top Democrat on the House Ethics Committee, said she thinks the panel’s report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) should be released.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane debate at state capitol heats up as temperatures cool down
item.description: Cooler temperatures mean hurricane season is almost behind us, but the debate in the state capital over storm recovery is just heating up.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump could 'revoke' China's free trade designation: Moolenaar
item.description: Moolenaar said tightening China's trade capabilities is "in line" with the president-elect's thinking.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Biden asks Congress for about $100 billion in disaster relief funding
item.description: The largest chunk of funding — about $40 billion — would be for FEMA's disaster relief fund, so that it has enough money to last through the coming year. But there are requests for 16 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: House Ethics Committee to meet Wednesday amid debate over Gaetz report
item.description: Members of the House Ethics Committee will meet Wednesday, a source confirmed to The Hill, as the panel weighs whether it should release its report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), whom President-elect Trump plans to nominate to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Some in the U.S. farm industry are alarmed by Trump's embrace of RFK Jr. and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump won landslide support in much of farm country, but his embrace of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his plan for a tariff fight with China alarms many farmers and agriculture experts.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hubbard County election judge faces felony charges for allegedly allowing unregistered voters to cast ballots
item.description: Prosecutors in Hubbard County say Timothy Michael Scouton — who was head judge at a precinct in rural Badoura Township — allowed 11 people to vote even though they had not registered.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NJ Transit, Amtrak testify before New Jersey lawmakers
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - Some palpable tension could be felt during Thursday's Assembly Transportation and Independent Authorities (TIA) Committee, which featured NJ Transit President Kevin Corbett and Amtrak CEO Stephen Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Gaetz once faced sex trafficking investigation by Justice Dept. he could now lead
item.description: Inside a private meeting of House Republicans, the news that Trump had chosen Gaetz to lead the Justice Department sent a shock.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump draft executive order would set up board to oust generals en masse: Report
item.description: If signed by Trump once he takes office, it could allow the quick removal of generals and admirals and purge the ranks of those the future commander-in-chief takes issue with for whatever reason.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
